claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I'll need to ask you a few questions. Remember that Child Benefit is assessed separately for each child, so I'll need to know about each child you're inquiring about.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have a child (or children) that you're responsible for? If yes, how old are they?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. My son Alex is 8 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. So you have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Since Alex is 8 years old (under 16), he would typically qualify as a "qualifying young person" for Child Benefit purposes. Now I need to ask about your circumstances:

**Are you responsible for Alex? This means do you live with him and are you the person looking after him?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. Alex lives with me and I look after him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good. Next question:

**Is Alex receiving any benefits in his own right, such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't receive any benefits in his own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Next question:

**Is Alex in any form of apprenticeship?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he's not in an apprenticeship. He's only 8, so he's just in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, that makes sense for an 8-year-old. Now I need to ask about your residency:

**Do you normally live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - in Manchester.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great. You've already confirmed that Alex lives with you and you look after him, so you meet the responsibility requirement. Now I need to ask:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. Now I need to ask about Alex's living situation:

**Is Alex currently in any of the following situations: in hospital, in local authority care, or in a foster care arrangement?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those apply. He lives at home with me full-time.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. One final question:

**Is the local council paying towards Alex's accommodation or maintenance costs?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, the council isn't paying anything towards his costs. I cover everything myself.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's a summary of why you qualify:

✅ **Alex is a qualifying child** - He's 8 years old (under 16), not receiving benefits in his own right, and not in an apprenticeship

✅ **You meet the residency requirements** - You normally live in the UK (Manchester)

✅ **You're responsible for Alex** - He lives with you and you look after him

✅ **No conflict with other claimants** - Nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him

✅ **No disqualifying circumstances** - Alex lives at home with you, and the local council isn't paying towards his costs

**Next steps to claim:**
- You can apply online or by post using form CH2
- You'll need to provide proof of identity for yourself and Alex
- You'll need Alex's birth certificate or adoption certificate
- Child Benefit is usually paid every 4 weeks

**Important note:** If you or your partner earn over £60,000 per year, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit Charge through your tax return. However, you can still claim Child Benefit - many people do so to protect their National Insurance credits for State Pension.

Do you have any questions about claiming or anything else I can help clarify?
